message: High risk of internal bleeding; antiplatelet + anticoagulant effect.
Critical drop in blood pressure (Fatal Hypotension).
Increased statin levels; risk of muscle pain (Rhabdomyolysis).
Risk of Serotonin Syndrome (confusion, rapid heart rate, seizures).
Risk of lactic acidosis and fluid/electrolyte imbalance.
Low potassium from diuretic can trigger Digoxin toxicity.
Increased theophylline levels; risk of tremors or seizures.
Risk of Hyperkalemia (critically high potassium levels).
Calcium reduces the absorption of thyroid hormone.
Dangerous increase in tricyclic antidepressant levels.
May increase carbamazepine levels leading to toxicity.
Severe respiratory depression and extreme sedation.
High risk of stomach ulcers and GI bleeding.
Beta-blocker may mask signs of low blood sugar (Hypoglycemia).
Omeprazole reduces the effectiveness of Clopidogrel.
Phenytoin speeds up the metabolism of the antibiotic.
Enhanced sedative effect of the benzodiazepine.
Increased risk of kidney damage and permanent hearing loss.
Risk of severe Methotrexate toxicity (bone marrow suppression).
NSAIDs can dangerously increase lithium levels in the blood.
Can cause severe bradycardia (slow heart rate) or heart block.
Enhanced anticoagulant effect; major bleeding risk.
Steroids increase blood sugar, opposing Metformin's effect.
Increased risk of upper gastrointestinal bleeding.
Increased risk of developing a skin rash.
Risk of kidney injury and severe low blood pressure.
Can interfere with digoxin clearance; monitor levels.
Extreme drowsiness and risk of breathing failure.
Diuretic reduces lithium excretion; risk of toxicity.
May increase risk of bruising and bleeding.
